info.title: Templarc
info.description:
Templarc is a **general-purpose template engine API** for provisioning, operational,
and text-generation tasks. It provides a product catalog of Jinja2 templates with a
parameter registry, inheritance chains, remote API data sources, dynamic form generation,
and full render history.

## Authentication

All endpoints require a valid JWT Bearer token. Obtain one via:
- `POST /auth/login` — JSON body `{username, password}` (preferred)
- `POST /auth/token` — OAuth2 form body (Swagger UI "Authorize" button)

## Three-Tier Parameter Scoping

Parameters are resolved at three scopes, applied in this order (later wins):

| Prefix | Scope | Defined in |
|--------|-------|-----------|
| `glob.*` | Organization-wide — every render | DB via `POST /parameters` |
| `proj.*` | Project-wide — all templates in a project | DB via `POST /parameters` |
| *(none)* | Template-local — this template only | `.j2` YAML frontmatter |

`glob.*` and `proj.*` values **cannot be overridden** by template-local parameters.

## Template Catalog Hierarchy

Templates form a tree for organization and parameter inheritance:
```
Router Provisioning (project)
  └── CPE (base template)
        └── Cisco
              └── Cisco 891  ← leaf template users select
```
Each child inherits its parent's template-local parameters and can override them.
The API catalog (`GET /catalog/{project_slug}`) returns this tree ready for UI rendering.

## Key Workflows

### Render a template
1. `GET /catalog/{slug}` — browse available templates
2. `GET /templates/{id}/resolve-params` — get the enriched parameter form definition
3. Fill in the form (remote data sources are already merged in step 2)
4. `POST /templates/{id}/render` — submit params, receive rendered output

### Manage templates (admin)
1. `POST /catalog/projects` — create a project (Git directory initialised)
2. `POST /templates` — create a template (writes `.j2` to Git)
3. `POST /parameters` — register parameters for the template
4. `POST /admin/git-sync/{project_id}` — import `.j2` files added directly in Git

## API Groups

| Tag | Base path | Description |
|-----|-----------|-------------|
| Authentication | `/auth` | Login, token management, user & secret CRUD |
| Catalog | `/catalog` | Project management and product catalog browsing |
| Templates | `/templates` | Template CRUD, variables analysis, inheritance chain |
| Parameters | `/parameters` | Parameter registry across all three scopes |
| Render | `/templates/{id}/render` | Template rendering and datasource resolution |
| Render History | `/render-history` | Render history storage and replay |
| Admin | `/admin` | Git sync, audit log, custom filters & objects |
| System | `/health` | Liveness / readiness probes |

post.summary: Obtain a JWT access token (OAuth2 form)
post.description: OAuth2-compatible form-based login. Accepts `Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded` with `username` and `password` fields. Kept for backward compatibility and OpenAPI 'Authorize' button support. Prefer `POST /auth/login` for new clients.  Rate-limited via global middleware (100 req/min per user).

post.summary: Obtain a JWT access token (JSON body)
post.description: Preferred login endpoint. Accepts a JSON body `{username, password}`. Dispatches to LDAP when `LDAP_SERVER` is configured, otherwise uses local bcrypt authentication.
Authenticate with a local bcrypt password. Returns 403 when `LDAP_SERVER` is configured to prevent LDAP bypass. Use this endpoint in environments without LDAP.

get.description: Return the username, org_id, and admin flag for the authenticated caller.

post.description: Create a new local user with a bcrypt-hashed password. The user is assigned to the caller's organization. Admin only.

get.description: Return metadata for all secrets in the caller's organization. Secret values are never included in the response. Admin only.

schema.title: Response List Secrets Auth Secrets Get

post.description: Create a new named secret for the caller's organization. For `db` type secrets the plaintext `value` is encrypted (Fernet/AES) before storage. The value is **never** returned by any API endpoint. Admin only.

delete.description: Permanently delete a secret by ID. Admin only.

get.description: Return all projects. Optionally filter by organization and/or a name search string.

schema.title: Response List Projects Catalog Projects Get

post.description: Create a new project. Initialises a Git subdirectory at `git_path` (defaults to `name`) with a .gitkeep commit so the directory exists before any templates are written.

get.description: Fetch a project by ID, including its full template hierarchy tree.

put.description: Partial update of project metadata. `name` and `organization_id` are immutable after creation.

get.description: Return the template hierarchy for a project as a nested tree. Only active templates are included. Nodes are ordered by sort_order then name within each level.

schema.title: Response Get Project Templates Catalog Projects  Project Id  Templates Get

get.description:
Return the full product catalog for a project, identified by its slug (`name` field). Intended for end-user selection UIs.

Each template entry includes:
- `breadcrumb` — ordered list of display names from root ancestor to this node
- `parameter_count` — active template-scope parameters registered
- `has_remote_datasources` — whether the `.j2` frontmatter declares `data_sources`
- `is_leaf` — `true` when the template has no active child templates

Both leaf templates (selectable by end users) and intermediate nodes (for hierarchical display) are included.
Return templates, optionally filtered by `project_id`. Only active templates are returned by default; pass `active_only=false` to include soft-deleted ones.

post.description:
Create a template record and write its initial `.j2` file to Git.

- `parent_template_id` must belong to the same project if provided.
- `git_path` is auto-generated as `{project.git_path}/{name}.j2` if omitted.
- If `content` is empty, a minimal frontmatter stub is written.

get.description: Fetch a template record by ID.

put.description:
Update template metadata and, when `content` is provided, write a new Git commit with the updated `.j2` file.

The response always includes a `suggested_parameters` list — variable references parsed from the new body, annotated with `is_registered` to show which are already in the parameter registry.

delete.description: Soft-delete a template by setting `is_active=False`. The `.j2` file is **not** removed from Git — history is preserved.

get.description: Parse the template's `.j2` file and return all Jinja2 variable references, each annotated with `is_registered` — whether a matching parameter exists in the registry (template-scope or project-scope).

schema.title: Response Get Template Variables Templates  Template Id  Variables Get

get.description:
Return the full parent chain for a template, from the root ancestor down to the requested template (inclusive), ordered root-first.

This reflects the catalog hierarchy used for parameter inheritance (not Jinja2 `extends`).

schema.title: Response Get Inheritance Chain Templates  Template Id  Inheritance Chain Get

get.description: Return a paginated list of parameters. Filter by scope, project, template, organization, or a search string (name/label).

post.description:
Create a new parameter. Scoping rules are enforced:
- `global`: name must start with `glob.`, `organization_id` required
- `project`: name must start with `proj.`, `project_id` required
- `template`: name must NOT start with `glob.` or `proj.`, `template_id` required

get.description: Fetch a single parameter by ID, including all its options.

put.description: Partial update of a parameter's metadata. Name and scope are immutable after creation.

delete.description: Soft-delete a parameter by setting is_active=False.

get.description: List all options for a select/multiselect parameter.

schema.title: Response List Options Parameters  Parameter Id  Options Get

post.description: Add an option to a select/multiselect parameter. Optionally specify condition_param + condition_value to show this option only when another parameter has a particular value.

delete.description: Delete a specific option from a parameter.

get.description: Returns the full enriched parameter set for a template, ready for the UI to build a dynamic input form.  Runs all `on_load` data sources in parallel and merges enrichments (prefill values, dropdown options, readonly overrides) into each parameter descriptor.

post.description: Render a template with provided parameter values.  Pass `?persist=false` to skip writing to `render_history` (useful for quick previews or CI dry-runs).  The response `output` includes the metadata header prepended to the rendered body.

parameters.name: persist

schema.description: Set false to skip render_history storage
schema.title: Persist

parameters.description: Set false to skip render_history storage

post.summary: Re-resolve datasources after a parameter change
post.description:
Call this when the user edits a form field.  Returns updated enrichments (prefill values, dropdown options) for any parameters whose datasources are triggered by the changed param.  Cascading triggers are resolved automatically with loop detection.

Response is a mapping of parameter name → enrichment object, e.g.:
```json
{"router.site_id": {"prefill": "42", "readonly": true, "source_id": "netbox"}}
```

schema.title: Response On Change Templates  Template Id  On Change  Param Name  Post

get.description: Return render history records, newest first, with optional filters.

get.summary: Get a single render history record
get.description: Fetch one render history record by ID. Includes the full resolved parameter set and the raw rendered output. Use this to inspect what was rendered and replay it via the re-render endpoint.

post.description: Re-runs a previous render using the exact same resolved parameters.  The template body is re-loaded from Git, so changes to the .j2 file are reflected in the output.  Pass `template_id` in the body to apply the stored params to a different template.

Scan the project's Git directory for ``.j2`` files and import any that are not yet registered in the database.

**Idempotent** — safe to run multiple times. Already-registered files are reported as ``already_registered`` and left unchanged.

**Fragment files** (``is_fragment: true`` in frontmatter) are counted in ``skipped_fragments`` and never imported as catalog templates.

Per-file errors do not abort the entire run; they are collected in the ``errors`` list of the response.

get.description:
Non-destructive comparison between Git files and DB records.

Reports:
- ``in_sync`` — file exists in both Git and the DB
- ``in_git_only`` — file exists in Git but has no DB record (candidate for import via the POST endpoint)
- ``in_db_only`` — DB record exists but the Git file is absent (orphaned or soft-deleted)
- ``fragment`` — file has ``is_fragment: true`` in its frontmatter (include-only, not a catalog template)

Does **not** modify any data.

Return a paginated list of audit log entries for all write operations (create, update, delete) on templates, parameters, projects, and secrets.\n\nSupports filtering by `user_sub`, `resource_type`, and date range.

parameters.name: date_from

schema.description: Include entries on or after this UTC timestamp
schema.title: Date From

parameters.description: Include entries on or after this UTC timestamp

parameters.name: date_to

schema.description: Include entries on or before this UTC timestamp
schema.title: Date To

parameters.description: Include entries on or before this UTC timestamp

post.description: Register a new custom Jinja2 filter. The Python code is validated through the sandbox (AST check + restricted exec + 100 ms test run) before being stored. On success, the filter becomes immediately available in the relevant project environment(s).

get.description: Return all active custom filters. Optionally filter by scope or project.

schema.title: Response List Filters Admin Filters Get

post.description: Validate and run a filter function in the sandbox without storing it. Useful for testing filter code from the UI before committing.

delete.description: Soft-delete a custom filter. The filter is deactivated immediately and the Jinja2 environment cache is invalidated. The response includes a ``used_in_templates`` list naming any templates that still reference this filter — these templates may fail to render until updated.

post.description: Register a Python class or factory function as a Jinja2 context object. The object will be available in templates as a global variable named after the ``name`` field. Code is sandbox-validated before storing.

get.description: Return all active custom objects. Optionally filter by project.

schema.title: Response List Objects Admin Objects Get

delete.description: Soft-delete a custom context object and invalidate the environment cache.

get.description: Returns 200 if the API and database are reachable, 503 otherwise.

resource_id.title: Resource Id
resource_id.description: Primary key of the affected row.

AuditLogOut.title: AuditLogOut
AuditLogOut.description: A single audit log entry representing one write operation.

CatalogProjectOut.title: CatalogProjectOut
CatalogProjectOut.description: Minimal project summary embedded in the catalog response.

CatalogResponse.title: CatalogResponse
CatalogResponse.description: Full product catalog response for a single project.

breadcrumb.title: Breadcrumb
breadcrumb.description: Ordered list of display_names from root to this template (inclusive).

parameter_count.title: Parameter Count
parameter_count.description: Count of active template-scope parameters registered for this template.

has_remote_datasources.title: Has Remote Datasources
has_remote_datasources.description: True if the template's .j2 frontmatter declares one or more data_sources.

is_leaf.title: Is Leaf
is_leaf.description: True when this template has no active child templates.

CatalogTemplateItem.title: CatalogTemplateItem
CatalogTemplateItem.description:
One template entry in the product catalog.

Includes enough information for a selection UI without requiring a
second API call:
  - breadcrumb: display_names from root ancestor down to this node
  - parameter_count: number of active template-scope parameters registered
  - has_remote_datasources: True if the .j2 frontmatter declares data_sources
  - is_leaf: True when the template has no active children

name.title: Name
name.description: Filter name used in templates as ``| name``. Lowercase + underscores only.

code.title: Code
code.description: Python source code containing exactly one function definition.

scope.title: Scope
scope.description: 'global' = available in all projects; 'project' = one project only.

project_id.title: Project Id
project_id.description: Required when scope='project'; must be None for scope='global'.

examples.description: Convert megabits to kilobits per second
examples.name: mb_to_kbps

used_in_templates.title: Used In Templates
used_in_templates.description: Names of templates that reference this filter (may need updating).

CustomFilterDeleteOut.title: CustomFilterDeleteOut
CustomFilterDeleteOut.description:
Response for DELETE /admin/filters/{id}.

The filter is soft-deleted regardless of usage.
``used_in_templates`` lists template names that still reference the
filter so the caller can take corrective action.

name.title: Name
name.description: Object name as available in templates (e.g. 'Router').

code.title: Code
code.description: Python source code: one class or factory function.

project_id.title: Project Id
project_id.description: Restrict to one project. None = available in all projects.

examples.description: Company-wide default values available in all templates
examples.name: Defaults

CustomObjectOut.title: CustomObjectOut
CustomObjectOut.description: A single custom context object entry.

EnrichedParameterOut.title: EnrichedParameterOut
EnrichedParameterOut.description: A resolved parameter enriched with any data-source prefills/options.

test_input.title: Test Input
test_input.description: Value passed as the sole argument to the filter function.

ok.title: Ok
ok.description: True if the function validated and ran without error.

output.title: Output
output.description: String representation of the return value.

current_params.title: Current Params
current_params.description: Current form state — all param values known so far

examples.description: Fully qualified domain name of the router
examples.label: Router Hostname
examples.name: router.hostname

examples.description: Fully qualified domain name of the router
examples.label: Router Hostname
examples.name: router.hostname

ParameterUpdate.title: ParameterUpdate
ParameterUpdate.description: All fields optional — only provided fields are updated.

git_path.title: Git Path
git_path.description: Subdirectory inside the templates repo. Defaults to `name` if omitted.

output_comment_style.title: Output Comment Style
output_comment_style.description: Comment prefix used in rendered output headers: #, !, //, or none

ProjectDetailOut.title: ProjectDetailOut
ProjectDetailOut.description: Project with its full template hierarchy tree.

template_id.title: Template Id
template_id.description: Override the original template. Useful for testing the same params against a new template version. Defaults to the original template.

RenderHistoryListOut.title: RenderHistoryListOut
RenderHistoryListOut.description: Paginated list of render history records.

output.title: Output
output.description: Full rendered output including the metadata header

render_id.title: Render Id
render_id.description: ID of the stored RenderHistory record; None when persist=false

examples.output:
! Rendered by admin at 2024-01-15T10:30:00Z
! Template: cisco_891
hostname edge-01.dc.company.com

params.title: Params
params.description: Template-local parameter values keyed by flat dot-notation name (e.g. 'router.hostname'). glob.* and proj.* keys are ignored.

notes.title: Notes
notes.description: Optional freeform render notes stored in history

name.title: Name
name.description: Unique name for this secret within the organization

secret_type.description: Storage back-end: env, vault, or db

value.title: Value
value.description: For env: the environment variable name to read. For db: the plaintext secret value (encrypted before storage). Not used for vault type.

examples.description: NetBox API token for data source enrichment
examples.name: netbox_api

examples.description: NetBox token fetched from HashiCorp Vault
examples.name: vault_netbox

SecretOut.title: SecretOut
SecretOut.description: Secret metadata — the plaintext value is never returned.

examples.description: NetBox API token for data source enrichment
examples.name: netbox_api

SyncErrorItem.title: SyncErrorItem
SyncErrorItem.description: A single per-file error encountered during a sync run.

SyncImportedTemplate.title: SyncImportedTemplate
SyncImportedTemplate.description: Summary of a template that was successfully imported from Git.

imported.title: Imported
imported.description: New templates created in this run.

already_registered.title: Already Registered
already_registered.description: Files already present in the DB — skipped.

skipped_fragments.title: Skipped Fragments
skipped_fragments.description: Files with `is_fragment: true` in their frontmatter — listed but not imported as catalog templates.

errors.title: Errors
errors.description: Per-file errors (invalid frontmatter, DB constraint violations, etc.).

imported_templates.title: Imported Templates
imported_templates.description: Templates successfully imported in this run.

SyncReport.title: SyncReport
SyncReport.description:
Result of POST /admin/git-sync/{project_id}.

Counts are mutually exclusive:
  scanned            = imported + already_registered + skipped_fragments + len(errors)

SyncStatusItem.title: SyncStatusItem
SyncStatusItem.description: Status of a single path in the drift comparison.

in_sync.title: In Sync
in_sync.description: Files that exist in both Git and the DB.

in_db_only.title: In Db Only
in_db_only.description: DB records whose git_path no longer exists on disk (orphaned or soft-deleted).

in_git_only.title: In Git Only
in_git_only.description: Git files that have not been imported into the DB.

skipped_fragments.title: Skipped Fragments
skipped_fragments.description: Git files identified as include-only fragments (is_fragment: true).

items.title: Items
items.description: Per-path breakdown of the drift status.

SyncStatusReport.title: SyncStatusReport
SyncStatusReport.description:
Result of GET /admin/git-sync/{project_id}/status.

Non-destructive drift check — compares DB records against Git files.

name.title: Name
name.description: Alphanumeric + underscores only, unique per project

parent_template_id.title: Parent Template Id
parent_template_id.description: Must belong to the same project if provided

content.title: Content
content.description: Initial body of the .j2 file. Frontmatter will be prepended if empty.

examples.author: admin
examples.content:
---
parameters:
  - name: router.hostname
    widget: text
    required: true
---
hostname {{ router.hostname }}
examples.name: cisco_891

content.title: Content
content.description: New .j2 file content (frontmatter + body). When provided, writes a new Git commit.

commit_message.title: Commit Message
commit_message.description: Git commit message. Defaults to 'Update template <name>' when content is provided.

suggested_parameters.title: Suggested Parameters
suggested_parameters.description: Variable references parsed from the updated template body. is_registered=False entries are candidates to add to the parameter registry.

is_registered.title: Is Registered
is_registered.description: True when a Parameter with this exact name exists in the registry (template-scope or project-scope).
